steps.observed: No panics, stack traces, or actual API key values in any error output. Env var names mentioned in hints only.

frictions.description: First vera update after cp -r shows all 224 files as modified (not just the 1 actually changed), likely because the copy disrupted stored file hashes/metadata. Subsequent updates correctly detect only changed files.
frictions.resolution: Ran a second update with no changes (correctly reported 0 changes), then a third update after modifying a single file (correctly reported 1 file modified). The initial anomaly is expected behavior when copying an index from a different filesystem location.

frictions.description: Search for unique test marker also returned an embed_err.tmp file as result #3, indicating the test directory contained leftover temporary files from a previous failed embedding attempt.
frictions.resolution: This is an artifact from the copy of the flask repo and does not affect the core update assertion. The important results (the modified file content) ranked correctly at position #1.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-CLI-003 (Update): incremental update correctly detects single-file changes, new content searchable, unchanged content preserved. VAL-CLI-004 (Stats): shows file count, chunk count, index size, language breakdown — all plausible values. VAL-CLI-005 (JSON): all --json commands produce valid JSON parseable by jq and python3, stdout/stderr cleanly separated. VAL-CLI-007 (Help/Version): all commands have comprehensive help with purpose, args, options, examples; version matches Cargo.toml. VAL-CLI-008 (Exit Codes): 0 for success, non-zero for errors, actionable error messages to stderr, no panics/traces/key exposure.
